stem: A small online quiz program lets users enter their age, the answer to a multiple-choice question and a comment.
model_answer: Defensive design anticipates misuse. The program should validate inputs (age must be an integer in a sensible range, the multiple-choice answer must be one of the allowed options, the comment must not exceed a maximum length); it should authenticate users (login + password) so people cannot quiz as someone else; and it should handle errors with a friendly message instead of crashing — for example a try/except around input conversion. These together keep the program reliable when a user makes a mistake or tries to break it.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Look for the term 'validation' and a concrete example, plus one other defensive technique (authentication, error handling).

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe three defensive design techniques the program should use. Give an example of each in this scenario.

mark_points.text: input validation (range / lookup / length / type) — applied to age or comment
error handling (catch exception so program does not crash)
